Abs Scalar.description:
Calculates absolute value of scalar

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: abs_scalar(-5.5, "result")

Absdiff.description:
Calculates absolute difference between mats

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID or scalar value

Example: absdiff("background")

Acos.description:
Calculates arc cosine (inverse cosine)

Params:
  value (float): Input value [-1, 1] or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name
  in_degrees (bool): Output in degrees [optional]

Example: acos(0.5, "result", true)

body:
# Full example with named args – only override what you need:
pipeline measure_brightness
    video_cap(${1:/dev/video0")
    adaptive_auto_brightness("/dev/video0}, ${2:
        exposure_prop="exposure"}, ${3:
        min_exposure=100}, ${4:max_exposure=20000}, ${5:
        analog_gain_prop="analogue_gain"}, ${6:
        min_analog_gain=100}, ${7:max_analog_gain=1600}, ${8:
        digital_gain_prop=""}, ${9:
        target=160}, ${10:
        brightness_mode="median"}, ${11:
        kp=0.9}, ${12:ki=0.01})
Adaptive Auto Brightness.description:
PID-based automatic brightness controller.  Adjusts up to three V4L2
control axes (exposure, analogue gain, digital gain) so that the
measured frame brightness converges to a configurable target.

Control is distributed across axes by priority: the first axis absorbs
as much correction as possible; overflow spills to the next axis.

V4L2 properties are written directly when their prop name is non-empty
(requires VISIONPIPE_V4L2_NATIVE_ENABLED).  Set any prop name to ""
to disable that axis; the item still returns the computed setpoints.

Returns: array [exposure_setpoint, analog_gain_setpoint, digital_gain_setpoint]

Params:
  source (any): Camera source: device path ("/dev/video0") or index
  exposure_prop (string): V4L2 control name for exposure (e.g. "exposure"); "" = disabled [optional]
  min_exposure (float): Minimum exposure setpoint (device units) [optional]
  max_exposure (float): Maximum exposure setpoint (device units) [optional]
  analog_gain_prop (string): V4L2 control name for analogue gain (e.g. "analogue_gain"); "" = disabled [optional]
  min_analog_gain (float): Minimum analogue gain (device units) [optional]
  max_analog_gain (float): Maximum analogue gain (device units) [optional]
  digital_gain_prop (string): V4L2 control name for digital gain (e.g. "digital_gain"); "" = disabled [optional]
  min_digital_gain (float): Minimum digital gain (device units) [optional]
  max_digital_gain (float): Maximum digital gain (device units) [optional]
  target (float): Target brightness 0-255 (default 128) [optional]
  brightness_mode (string): Brightness measurement: "median" (default) or "mean" [optional]
  roi_x (float): ROI left edge as fraction of frame width (0.0) [optional]
  roi_y (float): ROI top edge as fraction of frame height (0.0) [optional]
  roi_w (float): ROI width as fraction of frame width (1.0 = full) [optional]
  roi_h (float): ROI height as fraction of frame height (1.0 = full) [optional]
  kp (float): Proportional gain (default 0.8) [optional]
  ki (float): Integral gain (default 0.0) [optional]
  kd (float): Derivative gain (default 0.05) [optional]
  priority (string): Comma-separated axis priority order.
  Tokens: "exposure", "analog" (or "analogue"), "digital"
  Default: "exposure,analog,digital" [optional]
  deadband (float): Skip PID update and V4L2 writes when |error| < deadband (brightness units).
  Eliminates micro-adjustments at the cost of small steady-state offset.
  Default: 4.0 [optional]
  subsample (float): Pixel step for brightness sampling (1 = every pixel, 4 = every 4th pixel).
  Values > 1 switch to fast subsampled mean regardless of brightness_mode.
  Default: 4 (significant speedup on large frames with negligible accuracy loss) [optional]

Example: 
# Full example with named args – only override what you need:
pipeline measure_brightness
    video_cap("/dev/video0")
    adaptive_auto_brightness("/dev/video0",
        exposure_prop="exposure",
        min_exposure=100, max_exposure=20000,
        analog_gain_prop="analogue_gain",
        min_analog_gain=100, max_analog_gain=1600,
        digital_gain_prop="",
        target=160,
        brightness_mode="median",
        kp=0.9, ki=0.01)
end
exec_loop measure_brightness

Adaptive Auto Brightness Reset.description:
Resets the PID state of adaptive_auto_brightness.
Call this when the camera source changes or a manual control adjustment has been made externally.

Params:
  source (string): Source identifier to reset; omit to reset all sources [optional]

Example: adaptive_auto_brightness_reset()
adaptive_auto_brightness_reset("/dev/video0")

Adaptive Threshold.description:
Applies adaptive threshold using local neighborhood

Params:
  maxval (float): Maximum value [optional]
  method (string): Method: mean, gaussian [optional]
  type (string): Type: binary, binary_inv [optional]
  block_size (int): Neighborhood size (odd) [optional]
  c (float): Constant subtracted from mean [optional]

Example: adaptive_threshold(255, "gaussian", "binary", 11, 2)

Add.description:
Adds current mat with another mat or scalar

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID or scalar value
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]
  dtype (string): Output depth [optional]

Example: add("other_img")

Add Border.description:
Adds border around image

Params:
  top (int): Top border [optional]
  bottom (int): Bottom border [optional]
  left (int): Left border [optional]
  right (int): Right border [optional]
  type (string): Type: constant, replicate, reflect, wrap, reflect_101 [optional]
  color (string): Border color (for constant) [optional]

Example: add_border(10, 10, 10, 10, "constant", "white")

Add Weighted.description:
Weighted sum of two mats

Params:
  alpha (float): Weight for current mat
  other_cache (string): Second image cache ID
  beta (float): Weight for second mat
  gamma (float): Added scalar [optional]

Example: add_weighted(0.7, "overlay", 0.3, 0)

Agast.description:
Detects corners using AGAST algorithm

Params:
  threshold (int): Detection threshold [optional]
  nonmax_suppression (bool): Non-maximum suppression [optional]
  type (string): Type: 5_8, 7_12d, 7_12s, oast_9_16 [optional]

Example: agast(10, true, "oast_9_16")

Akaze.description:
Detects AKAZE features and computes descriptors

Params:
  threshold (float): Detector threshold [optional]
  n_octaves (int): Maximum octaves [optional]
  n_octave_layers (int): Layers per octave [optional]
  cache_id (string): Cache ID [optional]

Example: akaze(0.001, 4, 4, "akaze")

Apply Background Subtractor.description:
Applies background subtractor to current frame

Params:
  subtractor_cache (string): Subtractor cache ID
  learning_rate (float): Learning rate (-1 = auto) [optional]

Example: apply_background_subtractor("bg_sub", -1)

Apply Colormap.description:
Applies a colormap to grayscale image

Params:
  colormap (string): Colormap: autumn, bone, jet, winter, rainbow, ocean, summer, spring, cool, hsv, pink, hot, parula, magma, inferno, plasma, viridis, cividis, twilight, twilight_shifted, turbo, deepgreen [optional]

Example: apply_colormap("jet") | apply_colormap("viridis")

Approx Poly Dp.description:
Approximates contour with polygon

Params:
  epsilon (float): Approximation accuracy [optional]
  closed (bool): Closed curve [optional]
  cache_id (string): Cache ID of contours [optional]

Example: approx_poly_dp(0.02, true, "contours")

Arc Length.description:
Calculates contour perimeter

Params:
  closed (bool): Closed contour [optional]
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]

Example: arc_length(true, "contours")

Arrowed Line.description:
Draws an arrow

Params:
  x1 (int): Start X
  y1 (int): Start Y
  x2 (int): End X (arrow head)
  y2 (int): End Y (arrow head)
  color (string): Arrow color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]
  tip_length (float): Arrow tip length ratio [optional]

Example: arrowed_line(0, 0, 100, 100, "red", 2, 0.1)

Asin.description:
Calculates arc sine (inverse sine)

Params:
  value (float): Input value [-1, 1] or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name
  in_degrees (bool): Output in degrees [optional]

Example: asin(0.5, "result", true)

Assert.description:
Asserts condition and stops if false

Params:
  condition (bool): Condition to check
  message (string): Error message [optional]

Example: assert(true, "Mat should not be empty")

Atan.description:
Calculates arc tangent (inverse tangent)

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name
  in_degrees (bool): Output in degrees [optional]

Example: atan(1.0, "result", true)

Atan2.description:
Calculates arc tangent of y/x (2-argument form)

Params:
  y (float): Y value or variable name
  x (float): X value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name
  in_degrees (bool): Output in degrees [optional]

Example: atan2(y, x, "angle", true)

Auto Canny.description:
Automatic Canny edge detection using median-based thresholds

Params:
  sigma (float): Threshold adjustment factor [optional]

Example: auto_canny() | auto_canny(0.5)

Auto Exposure.description:
Software auto-exposure: analyses frame brightness over N samples and returns a computed exposure value within [min_exp, max_exp]

Params:
  min_exp (float): Minimum exposure value (device units)
  max_exp (float): Maximum exposure value (device units)
  samples (int): Number of brightness samples to accumulate before recalculating [optional]
  target (float): Target mean brightness (0-255) [optional]
  speed (float): Convergence speed / gain (0.0-1.0, higher = faster) [optional]
  roi_x (float): ROI x-offset as fraction of width (0.0-1.0) [optional]
  roi_y (float): ROI y-offset as fraction of height (0.0-1.0) [optional]
  roi_w (float): ROI width as fraction of width (0.0-1.0, 0 = full) [optional]
  roi_h (float): ROI height as fraction of height (0.0-1.0, 0 = full) [optional]

Example: exp = auto_exposure(50, 10000, 10, 128, 0.3)
v4l2_prop("/dev/video0", "Exposure (Absolute)", exp)

Auto Exposure Reset.description:
Resets accumulated auto-exposure state

Params:
  source (string): Source identifier to reset (omit to reset all) [optional]

Example: auto_exposure_reset() | auto_exposure_reset("/dev/video0")

Background Subtractor Knn.description:
Creates KNN background subtractor

Params:
  history (int): History length [optional]
  dist2_threshold (float): Distance threshold [optional]
  detect_shadows (bool): Detect shadows [optional]

Example: background_subtractor_knn(500, 400.0, true)

Background Subtractor Mog2.description:
Creates MOG2 background subtractor

Params:
  history (int): History length [optional]
  var_threshold (float): Variance threshold [optional]
  detect_shadows (bool): Detect shadows [optional]
  cache_id (string): Subtractor cache ID [optional]

Example: background_subtractor_mog2(500, 16.0, true, "bg_sub")

Bf Matcher.description:
Matches descriptors using brute-force matcher

Params:
  query_cache (string): Query descriptors cache ID
  train_cache (string): Train descriptors cache ID
  norm_type (string): Norm: l2, l1, hamming, hamming2 [optional]
  cross_check (bool): Cross-check matches [optional]
  k (int): K nearest neighbors [optional]
  ratio_thresh (float): Lowe's ratio threshold [optional]

Example: bf_matcher("orb1", "orb2", "hamming", false, 2, 0.75)

Bilateral Filter.description:
Applies bilateral filter (edge-preserving smoothing)

Params:
  d (int): Diameter of pixel neighborhood (neg = computed from sigma) [optional]
  sigma_color (float): Filter sigma in color space [optional]
  sigma_space (float): Filter sigma in coordinate space [optional]
  border (string): Border type [optional]

Example: bilateral_filter(9, 75, 75)

Bitwise And.description:
Bitwise AND operation

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID for second operand
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: bitwise_and("mask")

Bitwise Not.description:
Bitwise NOT (inversion) operation

Params:
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: bitwise_not()

Bitwise Or.description:
Bitwise OR operation

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID for second operand
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: bitwise_or("mask")

Bitwise Xor.description:
Bitwise XOR operation

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID for second operand
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: bitwise_xor("mask")

Blob From Image.description:
Create DNN blob from image for inference

Params:
  scale (float): Scale factor (default: 1/255) [optional]
  width (int): Target width (0 = original) [optional]
  height (int): Target height (0 = original) [optional]
  swap_rb (bool): Swap R and B channels [optional]

Example: blob_from_image(1/255.0, 640, 640, true)

Blur.description:
Applies simple averaging blur

Params:
  ksize (int): Kernel size [optional]

Example: blur(5)

Bounding Rect.description:
Calculates bounding rectangle of contours and draws them

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  color (string): Rectangle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: bounding_rect("contours", "green", 2)

Box Filter.description:
Applies box filter (averaging filter with option for unnormalized)

Params:
  ksize (int): Kernel size [optional]
  normalize (bool): Normalize by kernel area [optional]
  ddepth (int): Output depth (-1 = same as input) [optional]
  anchor_x (int): Anchor X (-1 = center) [optional]
  anchor_y (int): Anchor Y (-1 = center) [optional]

Example: box_filter(5) | box_filter(3, false)

Break.description: Breaks out of current loop (exec_loop, while)

Brightness Contrast.description:
Adjusts brightness and contrast

Params:
  brightness (float): Brightness adjustment (-100 to 100) [optional]
  contrast (float): Contrast multiplier (0.0 to 3.0) [optional]

Example: brightness_contrast(10, 1.2)

Brisk.description:
Detects BRISK features and computes descriptors

Params:
  thresh (int): AGAST detection threshold [optional]
  octaves (int): Detection octaves [optional]
  pattern_scale (float): Pattern scale [optional]
  cache_id (string): Cache ID [optional]

Example: brisk(30, 3, 1.0, "brisk")

Build Pyramid.description:
Builds a Gaussian pyramid and caches all levels

Params:
  max_level (int): Number of pyramid levels
  prefix (string): Cache prefix for levels [optional]

Example: build_pyramid(4, "pyr")  // Creates pyr_0, pyr_1, pyr_2, pyr_3

Button.description:
Creates a button-like trackbar that triggers when clicked

Params:
  name (string): Button name
  window (string): Window name
  clicked_var (string): Variable set when clicked

Example: button("Apply", "Control", "apply_clicked")

Cache.description:
Stores current image in cache with given ID

Params:
  cache_id (string): Cache identifier

Example: cache("my_image")

Cache If.description:
Caches current Mat only if condition is true

Params:
  condition (bool): Boolean condition value or variable
  cache_id (string): Cache identifier

Example: cache_if(is_valid, "good_frame")

Calc Back Project.description:
Calculates back projection of a histogram

Params:
  hist (string): Cache ID of histogram
  scale (float): Scale factor [optional]

Example: calc_back_project("hist")

Calc Hist.description:
Calculates histogram of the image

Params:
  channel (int): Channel to calculate (default: 0) [optional]
  hist_size (int): Number of histogram bins [optional]
  range_min (float): Minimum range value [optional]
  range_max (float): Maximum range value [optional]

Example: calc_hist(0, 256)

Calc Optical Flow Farneback.description:
Calculates dense optical flow using Farneback algorithm

Params:
  prev_cache (string): Previous frame cache ID
  pyr_scale (float): Pyramid scale [optional]
  levels (int): Number of pyramid levels [optional]
  winsize (int): Averaging window size [optional]
  iterations (int): Iterations at each level [optional]
  poly_n (int): Polynomial neighborhood size [optional]
  poly_sigma (float): Polynomial sigma [optional]
  cache_id (string): Flow cache ID [optional]

Example: calc_optical_flow_farneback("prev_frame", 0.5, 3, 15, 3, 5, 1.2, "flow")

Calc Optical Flow Pyr Lk.description:
Calculates sparse optical flow using Lucas-Kanade pyramid method

Params:
  prev_cache (string): Previous frame cache ID
  prev_points_cache (string): Previous points cache ID
  win_size (int): Search window size [optional]
  max_level (int): Maximum pyramid level [optional]
  cache_prefix (string): Output cache prefix [optional]

Example: calc_optical_flow_pyr_lk("prev_frame", "prev_points", 21, 3, "flow")

Calibrate Camera.description:
Calibrates camera from object/image points

Params:
  object_points_cache (string): Object points cache
  image_points_cache (string): Image points cache
  cache_prefix (string): Cache prefix for results [optional]

Example: calibrate_camera("obj_pts", "img_pts", "calib")

Canny.description:
Finds edges using Canny algorithm

Params:
  threshold1 (float): First threshold for hysteresis
  threshold2 (float): Second threshold for hysteresis
  aperture_size (int): Sobel aperture size (3, 5, 7) [optional]
  l2_gradient (bool): Use L2 norm for gradient [optional]

Example: canny(50, 150) | canny(100, 200, 5, true)

Cart To Polar.description:
Converts Cartesian coordinates to polar

Params:
  y_cache (string): Y component cache
  angle_in_degrees (bool): Angle in degrees [optional]
  cache_prefix (string): Output cache prefix [optional]

Example: cart_to_polar("y", true, "polar")

Ceil Scalar.description:
Rounds up to integer

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: ceil_scalar(3.1, "result")

Check Button Click.description:
Checks if button was clicked and resets the state

Params:
  name (string): Button name
  window (string): Window name
  var_name (string): Return value into this variable [optional]

Example: check_button_click("Apply", "Control", "was_clicked")

Checkbox.description:
Creates a checkbox using a trackbar bound to a boolean variable

Params:
  name (string): Checkbox name
  window (string): Window name
  var_name (string): Boolean variable name
  initial (bool): Initial state [optional]

Example: checkbox("Enable Filter", "Control", "filter_enabled", true)

Chessboard Detected Save.description:
Saves detected chessboard corners to a JSON file in the given directory (only when found)

Params:
  file_dir (string): Directory to save JSON files into
  corners_cache (string): Cache ID for detected corners [optional]
  pattern_width (int): Pattern inner-corner columns (for metadata) [optional]
  pattern_height (int): Pattern inner-corner rows (for metadata) [optional]

Example: chessboard_detected_save("/tmp/calib") | chessboard_detected_save("/tmp/calib", "corners", 9, 6)

Circle.description:
Draws a circle

Params:
  x (int): Center X
  y (int): Center Y
  radius (int): Radius
  color (string): Circle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness (-1 for filled) [optional]

Example: circle(100, 100, 50, "blue", -1)

Clahe.description:
Applies CLAHE (Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization)

Params:
  clip_limit (float): Contrast limit threshold [optional]
  tile_grid_size (int): Size of grid for tiles [optional]

Example: clahe(2.0, 8)

Clamp.description:
Clamps value to range [min, max]

Params:
  value (float): Value to clamp or variable name
  min (float): Minimum value
  max (float): Maximum value
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: clamp(value, 0, 255, "clamped")

Clear Cache.description:
Clears specified cache entry or all cache

Params:
  cache_id (string): Cache ID to clear (empty = all) [optional]

Example: clear_cache("my_image")

Clone.description:
Creates a deep copy of current mat

Params:
  cache_id (string): Cache ID for clone [optional]

Example: clone("backup")

Color Change.description:
Changes color of image region

Params:
  mask_cache (string): Mask cache ID
  red_mul (float): Red multiplier [optional]
  green_mul (float): Green multiplier [optional]
  blue_mul (float): Blue multiplier [optional]

Example: color_change("mask", 1.5, 1.0, 0.5)

Color Overlay.description:
Applies color overlay to image

Params:
  color (string): Overlay color [optional]
  alpha (float): Opacity (0-1) [optional]

Example: color_overlay("blue", 0.3)

Color Picker Control.description:
Creates 3 trackbars for BGR color selection

Params:
  prefix (string): Variable prefix for _b, _g, _r
  window (string): Window name
  initial_b (int): Initial blue value [optional]
  initial_g (int): Initial green value [optional]
  initial_r (int): Initial red value [optional]

Example: color_picker_control("color", "Color Picker", 255, 0, 0)

Compare Hist.description:
Compares two histograms

Params:
  other (string): Cache ID of other histogram
  method (string): Comparison method: correlation, chi_square, intersection, bhattacharyya, kl_div [optional]

Example: compare_hist("hist2", "correlation")

Connected Components.description:
Labels connected components in binary image

Params:
  connectivity (int): Connectivity: 4 or 8 [optional]

Example: connected_components(8)

Connected Components With Stats.description:
Labels components and computes statistics

Params:
  connectivity (int): Connectivity: 4 or 8 [optional]
  cache_prefix (string): Cache prefix for stats [optional]

Example: connected_components_with_stats(8, "cc")

Contour Area.description:
Calculates contour area

Params:
  oriented (bool): Return signed area [optional]
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]

Example: contour_area(false, "contours")

Control Panel.description:
Creates a control panel window with common image processing controls

Params:
  name (string): Control panel window name
  preset (string): Preset: threshold, blur, edge, morphology, color

Example: control_panel("Controls", "threshold")

Convert Maps.description:
Converts floating-point maps to fixed-point for faster remapping

Params:
  map1 (string): Cache ID of first input map
  map2 (string): Cache ID of second input map
  dstmap1type (int): Type of first output map [optional]

Example: convert_maps("map1", "map2")

Convert To.description:
Converts mat to different depth with optional scaling

Params:
  depth (string): Target depth: 8u, 8s, 16u, 16s, 32s, 32f, 64f
  alpha (float): Scale factor [optional]
  beta (float): Offset [optional]

Example: convert_to("32f", 1.0/255.0, 0)

Convex Hull.description:
Finds convex hull of contours

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  color (string): Hull color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: convex_hull("contours", "green", 2)

Convexity Defects.description:
Finds convexity defects of contour

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]

Example: convexity_defects("contours")

Copy Cache.description:
Copies one cache entry to another

Params:
  source (string): Source cache ID
  dest (string): Destination cache ID

Example: copy_cache("src", "dst")

Copy Make Border.description:
Adds border around the image

Params:
  top (int): Top border width
  bottom (int): Bottom border width
  left (int): Left border width
  right (int): Right border width
  border_type (string): Type: constant, replicate, reflect, wrap, reflect_101 [optional]
  value (int): Border value for constant type [optional]

Example: copy_make_border(10, 10, 10, 10, "constant", 255)

Copy To.description:
Copies current mat to destination with optional mask

Params:
  dest_cache (string): Destination cache ID
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask cache ID [optional]

Example: copy_to("dest", "mask")

Corner Harris.description:
Detects corners using Harris corner detector

Params:
  block_size (int): Neighborhood size [optional]
  ksize (int): Sobel aperture [optional]
  k (float): Harris parameter [optional]
  threshold (float): Response threshold (0-1) [optional]

Example: corner_harris(2, 3, 0.04, 0.01)

Corner Min Eigen Val.description:
Calculates minimum eigenvalue for corner detection

Params:
  block_size (int): Neighborhood size [optional]
  ksize (int): Sobel aperture [optional]

Example: corner_min_eigen_val(3, 3)

Corner Sub Pix.description:
Refines corner locations to sub-pixel accuracy

Params:
  win_size (int): Half window size [optional]
  zero_zone (int): Half dead zone size (-1 to disable) [optional]
  max_count (int): Maximum iterations [optional]
  epsilon (float): Convergence threshold [optional]

Example: corner_sub_pix(5, -1, 30, 0.001)

Cos.description:
Calculates cosine of value

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name
  in_degrees (bool): Input in degrees [optional]

Example: cos(angle, "result", true)

Create Mat.description:
Creates a new mat with specified dimensions and type

Params:
  rows (int): Number of rows
  cols (int): Number of columns
  type (string): Type: 8uc1, 8uc3, 32fc1, etc. [optional]
  value (float): Initial value [optional]

Example: create_mat(480, 640, "8uc3", 0)

Create Merge Mertens.description:
Creates Mertens exposure fusion algorithm

Params:
  contrast_weight (float): Contrast weight [optional]
  saturation_weight (float): Saturation weight [optional]
  exposure_weight (float): Exposure weight [optional]

Example: create_merge_mertens(1.0, 1.0, 0.0)

Create Trackbar.description:
Creates a trackbar and attaches it to window

Params:
  name (string): Trackbar name
  window (string): Window name
  max_value (int): Maximum value
  initial (int): Initial value [optional]

Example: create_trackbar("Threshold", "Output", 255, 128)

Crop.description:
Crops a rectangular region from the image

Params:
  x (int): Left X coordinate
  y (int): Top Y coordinate
  width (int): Width of crop region
  height (int): Height of crop region

Example: crop(100, 100, 200, 200)

Cvt Color.description:
Converts color space of the current frame

Params:
  code (string): Conversion code: bgr2gray, bgr2hsv, bgr2lab, yuv2bgr, uyvy2bgr, nv122bgr, bayerbg2bgr, etc.

Example: cvt_color("bgr2gray") | cvt_color("uyvy2bgr")

Dct.description:
Discrete Cosine Transform

Params:
  flags (string): Flags: none, inverse, rows [optional]

Example: dct("none")

Debayer.description:
Applies Bayer pattern demosaicing (debayering) to single-channel raw sensor data

Params:
  pattern (string): Bayer tile pattern: RGGB, BGGR, GBRG, GRBG, or "auto" to detect from the active camera [optional]
  algorithm (string): Demosaic algorithm: bilinear, vng, ea [optional]
  output (string): Output format: bgr or rgb [optional]
  bit_shift (int): Right-shift for 10/12-bit packed inputs (e.g. 2 for 10-bit in 16-bit container) [optional]

Example: debayer() | debayer("auto") | debayer("RGGB", "ea", "bgr", 2)

Debug.description:
Prints debug information about current Mat

Params:
  label (string): Debug label [optional]

Example: debug("After blur")

Decode Yolo.description:
Decode YOLO model output to detections

Params:
  version (string): YOLO version: v5, v8, v10, v11 [optional]
  conf_thresh (float): Confidence threshold [optional]
  orig_width (int): Original image width [optional]
  orig_height (int): Original image height [optional]
  input_size (int): Model input size [optional]

Example: decode_yolo("v8", 0.25) -> "detections"

Decrement Var.description:
Decrements a variable by specified amount

Params:
  name (string): Variable name
  amount (float): Decrement amount [optional]

Example: decrement_var("counter", 1)

Destroy Window.description:
Destroys specified window

Params:
  name (string): Window name

Example: destroy_window("Output")

Detail Enhance.description:
Enhances image details

Params:
  sigma_s (float): Spatial sigma [optional]
  sigma_r (float): Range sigma [optional]

Example: detail_enhance(10, 0.15)

Dft.description:
Discrete Fourier Transform

Params:
  flags (string): Flags: none, inverse, scale, rows, complex_output, real_output [optional]
  nonzero_rows (int): Non-zero input rows (0 = all) [optional]

Example: dft("complex_output", 0)

Dilate.description:
Dilates image using a structuring element

Params:
  kernel_size (int): Kernel size [optional]
  shape (string): Shape: rect, cross, ellipse [optional]
  iterations (int): Number of iterations [optional]

Example: dilate(3) | dilate(5, "ellipse", 2)

Disparity To Depth.description:
Converts disparity to depth using baseline and focal length

Params:
  baseline (float): Stereo baseline (distance between cameras)
  focal_length (float): Camera focal length in pixels

Example: disparity_to_depth(0.12, 700)

Disparity Wls Filter.description:
Applies WLS filter to disparity map (requires ximgproc)

Params:
  lambda (float): Regularization parameter [optional]
  sigma (float): Sigma color parameter [optional]

Example: disparity_wls_filter(8000, 1.5)

Display If.description:
Displays Mat only if condition is true

Params:
  condition (bool): Boolean condition value or variable
  window_name (string): Window name [optional]

Example: display_if(show_debug, "Debug View")

Distance Transform.description:
Calculates distance to nearest zero pixel

Params:
  distance_type (string): Type: l1, l2, c [optional]
  mask_size (int): Mask size: 3, 5, 0 (precise) [optional]

Example: distance_transform("l2", 5)

Divide.description:
Element-wise division

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID or scalar value
  scale (float): Scale factor [optional]

Example: divide("normalizer", 1.0)

Draw Chessboard Corners.description:
Draws detected chessboard corners on image

Params:
  pattern_width (int): Pattern width
  pattern_height (int): Pattern height
  corners_cache (string): Corners cache ID [optional]

Example: draw_chessboard_corners(9, 6, "corners")

Draw Classification.description:
Draw classification results on the current frame

Params:

  labels (string): Path to labels file [optional]

Example: draw_classification("predictions", labels="imagenet.txt")

Draw Contours.description:
Draws contours on image

Draw Crosshair.description:
Draws crosshair at center or specified position

Params:

  y (int): Center Y (-1 for image center) [optional]
  size (int): Crosshair size [optional]
  color (string): Crosshair color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Draw Detections.description:
Draw detection boxes on the current frame

Params:
  detections (string): Cache ID of detections matrix

  show_conf (bool): Show confidence scores [optional]
  thickness (int): Box line thickness [optional]

Example: draw_detections("detections", labels="coco.names")

Draw Grid.description:
Draws grid lines on image

Params:
  cell_width (int): Grid cell width [optional]

  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Draw Histogram.description:
Draws histogram visualization

Params:
  width (int): Histogram width [optional]

  color (string): Histogram color [optional]
  background (string): Background color [optional]

Draw Keypoints.description:
Draws keypoints on image

Example: draw_keypoints("green", true)

Draw Labeled Rect.description:
Draws rectangle with text label

  width (int): Width
  height (int): Height
  label (string): Label text

Draw Marker.description:
Draws a marker at specified position

Params:

Draw Matches.description:
Draws feature matches between two images

  img2_cache (string): Second image cache ID

Draw Optical Flow.description:
Visualizes optical flow as arrows or HSV

Draw Rotated Rect.description:

  angle (float): Rotation angle in degrees

Edge Preserving Filter.description:

Eigen.description:
Computes eigenvalues and eigenvectors of symmetric matrix

Elapsed Time.description:

Ellipse.description:

Equalize Hist.description: Equalizes histogram of grayscale image or Y channel of color

Erode.description:
Erodes image using a structuring element

Params:
  kernel_size (int): Kernel size [optional]
  shape (string): Shape: rect, cross, ellipse [optional]
  iterations (int): Number of iterations [optional]

Example: erode(3) | erode(5, "ellipse", 2)

Exp Scalar.description:
Calculates e^x

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: exp_scalar(2, "result")

Extract Channel.description:
Extracts a single channel from the image

Params:

Example: extract_channel(0)  // Extract blue channel

Eye.description:
Creates an identity matrix

Params:
  size (int): Matrix size (square)
  type (string): Type: 32fc1, 64fc1 [optional]

Example: eye(3, "64fc1")

Fast.description:
Detects corners using FAST algorithm

Params:
  threshold (int): Detection threshold [optional]

Fast Nl Means Denoising.description:
Non-local means denoising for grayscale images

Params:

  template_size (int): Template patch size [optional]
  search_size (int): Search window size [optional]

Example: fast_nl_means_denoising(10, 7, 21)

Fast Nl Means Denoising Colored.description:
Non-local means denoising for color images

Params:

Fill Convex Poly.description:

Fill Poly.description:
Fills polygon from cached points

Find Chessboard Corners.description:
Finds chessboard corners for calibration

Find Circles Grid.description:
Finds circle grid centers for calibration

  cache_id (string): Cache ID for centers [optional]

Find Contours.description:
Finds contours in binary image

Params:

  method (string): Method: none, simple, tc89_l1, tc89_kcos [optional]
  cache_id (string): Cache ID for contours [optional]

Example: find_contours("external", "simple", "contours")

Find Essential Mat.description:
Calculates essential matrix from point correspondences

Params:
  points1_cache (string): First image points cache
  points2_cache (string): Second image points cache

  method (string): Method: ransac, lmeds [optional]

Example: find_essential_mat("pts1", "pts2", "K", "ransac", 0.999, 1.0, "E")

Find Fundamental Mat.description:
Calculates fundamental matrix from point correspondences

Params:
  points1_cache (string): First image points cache

  method (string): Method: 7point, 8point, ransac, lmeds [optional]
  ransac_reproj_threshold (float): RANSAC threshold [optional]

Find Homography.description:
Finds perspective transformation between two planes

Params:

  dst_points_cache (string): Destination points cache ID

Floor Scalar.description:

Fps.description:

Good Features To Track.description:
Finds corners using Shi-Tomasi corner detection

  min_distance (float): Minimum distance between corners [optional]
  block_size (int): Neighborhood size [optional]

  k (float): Harris parameter [optional]

Example: good_features_to_track(100, 0.01, 10)

Gpu Debayer.description:
GPU-accelerated Bayer demosaicing. Default algorithm "malvar" runs a native OpenCL kernel implementing the Malvar-He-Cutler high-quality linear interpolation (2004), adapted from cldemosaic (https://github.com/nevion/cldemosaic). Also supports "bilinear", "vng", and "ea" via OpenCV T-API. Falls back to CPU when OpenCL is unavailable.

Params:
  pattern (string): Bayer tile pattern: RGGB, BGGR, GBRG, GRBG, or "auto" [optional]
  algorithm (string): Demosaic algorithm: malvar (native OpenCL, default), bilinear, vng, ea [optional]
  output (string): Output color order: bgr or rgb [optional]
  bit_shift (int): Right-shift for 10/12-bit packed inputs (e.g. 2 for 10-bit in 16-bit container) [optional]

Example: gpu_debayer("auto") | gpu_debayer("RGGB", "malvar") | gpu_debayer("RGGB", "ea", "bgr", 2)

Gpu Horizon Lock.description:
GPU-accelerated horizon lock (rotation-only stabilization). Same algorithm as horizon_lock() but with feature detection, optical flow, and the corrective warp dispatched through OpenCL (native kernel with T-API fallback). Falls back to CPU if OpenCL is unavailable.

Params:
  delta_smooth (float): EMA for delta noise filter [0,1) (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame angle decay (0,1] (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min corner distance in px (default 10) [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop borders (default true) [optional]
  reset (bool): Reset state (default false) [optional]

Example: video_cap("/dev/video0") | gpu_horizon_lock()
video_cap("/dev/video0") | gpu_horizon_lock(0.5, 0.997, 300)

Gpu Matrix Mul.description:
GPU-accelerated general matrix multiplication (GEMM). Computes: dst = alpha * op(A) * op(B) + beta * C, where A = currentMat, B and C (optional) are loaded from cache. op() is controlled by the flags parameter. Input matrices are automatically converted to CV_32F. Primary: native OpenCL tiled GEMM kernel. Fallback: OpenCV T-API (cv::gemm + UMat). CPU fallback: cv::gemm.

Params:
  src2_cache (string): Cache key for matrix B (second operand)
  alpha (float): Scale factor for the product A*B [optional]
  src3_cache (string): Cache key for addend matrix C (empty = no addend) [optional]
  beta (float): Scale factor for C [optional]
  flags (string): Transpose flags: "none", "at" (transpose A), "bt" (transpose B), "atbt" [optional]

Example: gpu_matrix_mul("B") | gpu_matrix_mul("W", 1.0, "", 0.0, "none") | gpu_matrix_mul("B", 2.0, "C", 1.0, "at")

Gpu Transform.description:
GPU-accelerated per-pixel colour/channel linear transform. Applies a cached float matrix M to every pixel vector:   dst_pixel = M * pixel        (M.cols == src_channels)   dst_pixel = M * [pixel|1]    (M.cols == src_channels+1, with bias). Primary: native OpenCL kernel. Fallback: OpenCV T-API (cv::transform + UMat). CPU fallback: cv::transform.

Params:
  matrix_cache (string): Cache key of the float transform matrix (CV_32F, m x n or m x n+1)
  depth (string): Output depth: "8u", "16u", "32f", or "same" (default) [optional]

Example: gpu_transform("CCM") | gpu_transform("CCM3x3", "8u")

Gpu Video Stabilize.description:
GPU-accelerated full 2-D video stabilizer (translation + rotation). Same algorithm as video_stabilize() with feature detection, optical flow, and the corrective warp dispatched through OpenCL (native kernel with T-API fallback). Falls back to CPU if OpenCL is unavailable.

Params:
  delta_smooth (float): EMA for delta noise filter [0,1) (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame decay (0,1] (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min corner distance in px (default 10) [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop borders (default true) [optional]
  reset (bool): Reset state (default false) [optional]

Example: video_cap("/dev/video0") | gpu_video_stabilize()
video_cap("/dev/video0") | gpu_video_stabilize(0.5, 0.997)
video_cap("/dev/video0") | gpu_video_stabilize(0.5, 1.0)

Horizon Lock.description:
Keeps the frame steady against rotational motion (horizon stabilizer). Tracks feature points across frames, estimates per-frame rotation with RANSAC partial-affine estimation, smooths the per-frame delta to reduce noise, integrates into an accumulated angle, and applies the full counter-rotation every frame — no lag, no overshoot.

Params:
  delta_smooth (float): EMA coefficient for raw delta noise filter [0,1). Higher = smoother, slower initial response (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame decay of accumulated angle (0,1]. 1.0 = hard lock; ~0.997 = soft lock that accepts very slow tilts (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners to track (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality level (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min distance between corners in px (default 10) [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop to hide rotation borders (default true) [optional]
  reset (bool): Reset accumulated state and restart (default false) [optional]

Example: video_cap("/dev/video0") | horizon_lock()
// soft lock — accepts very slow intentional tilts:
video_cap("/dev/video0") | horizon_lock(0.5, 0.997, 200, 0.01, 10, true)
// hard lock — fights all rotation permanently:
video_cap("/dev/video0") | horizon_lock(0.5, 1.0)

Element-wise minimum min(A, B) (cv::min, SIMD-accelerated).

Params:
  mat_a (mat): First matrix
  mat_b (mat): Second matrix

Example: darkest = matrix_min(frame_a, frame_b)

Matrix Mul.description:
Element-wise (Hadamard) multiplication A ⊙ B (cv::multiply). For true matrix multiplication use matrix_gemm.

Params:
  mat_a (mat): First matrix
  mat_b (mat): Second matrix

Example: product = matrix_mul(mask, frame)

Matrix Normalize.description:
Normalize matrix values to [alpha, beta] range using cv::normalize. norm_type selects the normalization method: "minmax" (default) maps the min/max to [alpha, beta]; "l1", "l2", "inf" normalise by the corresponding vector norm.

Params:
  mat (mat): Input matrix
  alpha (float): Lower bound / norm target (default 0) [optional]
  beta (float): Upper bound (default 255, ignored for non-minmax norms) [optional]
  norm_type (string): "minmax" | "l1" | "l2" | "inf" (default "minmax") [optional]

Example: norm = matrix_normalize(depth_map)
norm = matrix_normalize(feature_map, 0, 1, "l2")

Matrix Scale.description:
Multiply every element of the matrix by a scalar factor (cv::multiply with Scalar — SIMD-accelerated). Useful for halving frame brightness, normalizing depth maps, etc.

Params:
  mat (mat): Input matrix
  factor (float): Scale factor

Example: half = matrix_scale(frame, 0.5)
bright = matrix_scale(frame, 2.0)

Matrix Sub.description:
Element-wise subtraction A - B (cv::subtract, SIMD-accelerated).

Params:
  mat_a (mat): Minuend matrix
  mat_b (mat): Subtrahend matrix

Example: diff = matrix_sub(frame_a, frame_b)

Matrix Transpose.description:
Transpose a matrix Aᵀ (cv::transpose, SIMD-accelerated).

Params:
  mat (mat): Input matrix

Example: t = matrix_transpose(homography)

Max Scalar.description:
Returns maximum of two values

Params:
  a (float): First value or variable name
  b (float): Second value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: max_scalar(a, b, "max_val")

Median Blur.description:
Applies median blur (effective for salt-and-pepper noise)

Params:
  ksize (int): Kernel size (odd number) [optional]

Example: median_blur(5)

Merge Channels.description:
Merges separate channels into a multi-channel image

Params:
  channels (array): Array of cache IDs for channels

Example: merge_channels(["ch_0", "ch_1", "ch_2"])

Merge Exposures.description:
Merges multiple exposures using Mertens fusion

Params:
  images_cache (string): Comma-separated image cache IDs

Example: merge_exposures("exp1,exp2,exp3")

Min Area Rect.description:
Finds minimum area rotated rectangle

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  color (string): Rectangle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: min_area_rect("contours", "green", 2)

Min Enclosing Circle.description:
Finds minimum enclosing circle

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  color (string): Circle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: min_enclosing_circle("contours", "green", 2)

Min Enclosing Triangle.description:
Finds minimum enclosing triangle

Params:
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  color (string): Triangle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: min_enclosing_triangle("contours", "green", 2)

Min Max Loc.description:
Finds minimum and maximum values and their locations

Params:
  cache_prefix (string): Cache prefix for results [optional]

Example: min_max_loc("minmax")

Min Scalar.description:
Returns minimum of two values

Params:
  a (float): First value or variable name
  b (float): Second value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: min_scalar(a, b, "min_val")

Mix Channels.description:
Copies specified channels from input to output

Params:
  from_to (array): Pairs of [src_channel, dst_channel]
  output_channels (int): Number of output channels [optional]

Example: mix_channels([0, 2, 2, 0])  // Swap B and R

Model Get Outputs.description:
Get all DNN outputs as array of [name, matrix] pairs

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier

Example: model_get_outputs("yolo")

Model Infer.description:
Run inference on the current frame using a loaded model

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier

Example: model_infer("yolo") -> "output"

Model Info.description:
Get information about a loaded model

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier

Example: model_info("yolo")

Model Output.description:
Get specific output layer from model inference

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier
  layer (string): Output layer name

Example: model_output("yolo", "output0")

Model Output Matrix.description:
Get raw DNN output as Matrix (2D tensor)

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier
  layer (any): Layer name or index [optional]

Example: model_output_matrix("detector", "output0") -> my_matrix

Model Output Vector.description:
Get raw DNN output as Vector (1D tensor)

Params:
  id (string): Model identifier
  layer (any): Layer name or index [optional]

Example: model_output_vector("classifier", 0) -> my_vector

Moments.description:
Calculates image moments

Params:
  binary (bool): Treat as binary image [optional]

Example: moments() | moments(true)

Morphology Ex.description:
Performs advanced morphological operations

Params:
  op (string): Operation: open, close, gradient, tophat, blackhat, hitmiss
  kernel_size (int): Kernel size [optional]
  shape (string): Shape: rect, cross, ellipse [optional]
  iterations (int): Number of iterations [optional]

Example: morphology_ex("open", 5) | morphology_ex("gradient", 3, "ellipse")

Mosaic.description:
Creates a mosaic/grid from multiple cached images

Params:
  images (array): Array of cache IDs
  cols (int): Number of columns (default: auto) [optional]
  cell_width (int): Cell width (default: auto) [optional]
  cell_height (int): Cell height (default: auto) [optional]

Example: mosaic(["img1", "img2", "img3", "img4"], 2)

Move Window.description:
Moves window to specified position

Params:
  name (string): Window name
  x (int): X position
  y (int): Y position

Example: move_window("Output", 100, 100)

Mul Spectrums.description:
Per-element multiplication of two Fourier spectrums

Params:
  other_cache (string): Second spectrum cache ID
  flags (string): Flags: none, rows, conj [optional]

Example: mul_spectrums("kernel_dft", "none")

Multi Tracker.description:
Creates multi-object tracker

Params:
  tracker_type (string): Tracker type: kcf, csrt, mil [optional]
  cache_id (string): Multi-tracker cache ID [optional]

Example: multi_tracker("csrt", "multi_tracker")

Multiply.description:
Element-wise multiplication

Params:
  operand (string): Cache ID or scalar value
  scale (float): Scale factor [optional]

Example: multiply("mask", 1.0)

Named Window.description:
Creates a named window

Params:
  name (string): Window name
  flags (string): Flags: normal, autosize, fullscreen, freeratio, keepratio [optional]

Example: named_window("Output", "normal")

Nms Boxes.description:
Apply Non-Maximum Suppression to detections

Params:
  iou_thresh (float): IoU threshold for NMS [optional]
  conf_thresh (float): Confidence threshold [optional]

Example: nms_boxes(0.45, 0.25)

Norm.description:
Calculates matrix norm

Params:
  norm_type (string): Type: l1, l2, linf, l2sqr [optional]
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: norm("l2")

Normalize.description:
Normalizes array values

Params:
  alpha (float): Lower range boundary or norm value [optional]
  beta (float): Upper range boundary [optional]
  norm_type (string): Type: minmax, l1, l2, linf [optional]
  dtype (string): Output depth: -1, 8u, 32f [optional]
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask [optional]

Example: normalize(0, 255, "minmax", "8u")

Normalize Disparity.description:
Normalizes disparity map for visualization

Params:
  min_val (float): Minimum value [optional]
  max_val (float): Maximum value [optional]

Example: normalize_disparity(0, 255)

Normalize Mean Std.description:
Normalize image with mean and std (ImageNet-style)

Params:
  mean (array): Per-channel mean values [R, G, B]
  std (array): Per-channel std values [R, G, B]

Example: normalize_mean_std([0.485, 0.456, 0.406], [0.229, 0.224, 0.225])

Ones.description:
Creates a mat filled with ones

Params:
  rows (int): Number of rows
  cols (int): Number of columns
  type (string): Type: 8uc1, 8uc3, 32fc1, etc. [optional]

Example: ones(480, 640, "32fc1")

Orb.description:
Detects ORB features and computes descriptors

Params:
  n_features (int): Maximum features [optional]
  scale_factor (float): Pyramid scale factor [optional]
  n_levels (int): Pyramid levels [optional]
  edge_threshold (int): Edge threshold [optional]
  cache_id (string): Cache ID for descriptors [optional]

Example: orb(500, 1.2, 8, 31, "orb")

Otsu Threshold.description:
Applies Otsu's automatic threshold

Params:
  maxval (float): Maximum value [optional]
  type (string): Type: binary, binary_inv [optional]

Example: otsu_threshold() | otsu_threshold(255, "binary_inv")

Overlay Image.description:
Overlays cached image on current image

Params:
  overlay_cache (string): Overlay image cache ID
  x (int): Top-left X position
  y (int): Top-left Y position
  alpha (float): Opacity (0-1) [optional]

Example: overlay_image("logo", 10, 10, 0.5)

Pencil Sketch.description:
Creates pencil sketch effect

Params:
  sigma_s (float): Spatial sigma [optional]
  sigma_r (float): Range sigma [optional]
  shade_factor (float): Shade factor [optional]
  colored (bool): Return colored version [optional]

Example: pencil_sketch(60, 0.07, 0.02, false)

Perspective Transform Points.description:
Applies perspective transformation to points

Params:
  points_cache (string): Points cache ID
  matrix_cache (string): Transformation matrix cache ID
  output_cache (string): Output points cache ID [optional]

Example: perspective_transform_points("points", "H", "output")

Phase.description:
Calculates phase angle of 2D vectors

Params:
  y_cache (string): Y component cache
  angle_in_degrees (bool): Return angle in degrees [optional]

Example: phase("y", true)

Point Polygon Test.description:
Tests point-contour relationship

Params:
  x (float): Point X coordinate
  y (float): Point Y coordinate
  cache_id (string): Contour cache ID [optional]
  measure_dist (bool): Measure signed distance [optional]

Example: point_polygon_test(100, 100, "contours", true)

Polar To Cart.description:
Converts polar coordinates to Cartesian

Params:
  angle_cache (string): Angle component cache
  angle_in_degrees (bool): Angle in degrees [optional]
  cache_prefix (string): Output cache prefix [optional]

Example: polar_to_cart("angle", true, "cart")

Poll Key.description: Polls for key without blocking and returns the key code

Polylines.description:
Draws polylines from cached points

Params:
  points_cache (string): Points cache ID
  closed (bool): Close polyline [optional]
  color (string): Line color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness [optional]

Example: polylines("points", true, "green", 2)

Pow.description:
Raises every element to a power

Params:
  power (float): Exponent value

Example: pow(2.0)

Pow Scalar.description:
Raises scalar to power

Params:
  base (float): Base value or variable name
  exponent (float): Exponent value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: pow_scalar(2, 8, "result")

Print.description:
Prints message to console

Params:
  message (string): Message to print

Example: print("Processing complete")

Project Points.description:
Projects 3D points to image plane

Params:
  object_points (string): 3D object points cache
  rvec (string): Rotation vector cache
  tvec (string): Translation vector cache
  cam_matrix (string): Camera matrix cache
  dist_coeffs (string): Distortion coeffs cache
  cache_id (string): Output 2D points cache [optional]

Example: project_points("obj_pts", "rvec", "tvec", "K", "D", "proj_pts")

Promote Global.description:
Promotes a local cache entry to global scope

Params:
  cache_id (string): Cache identifier to promote

Example: promote_global("my_image")

Put Text.description:
Draws text on image

Params:
  text (string): Text to draw
  x (int): Bottom-left X
  y (int): Bottom-left Y
  font (string): Font: simplex, plain, duplex, complex, triplex, complex_small, script_simplex, script_complex, italic [optional]
  scale (float): Font scale [optional]
  color (string): Text color [optional]
  thickness (int): Text thickness [optional]

Example: put_text("Hello", 10, 30, "simplex", 1.0, "white", 2)

Pyr Down.description:
Blurs and downsamples image (Gaussian pyramid down)

Params:
  dst_width (int): Output width (default: half) [optional]
  dst_height (int): Output height (default: half) [optional]

Example: pyr_down() | pyr_down(320, 240)

Pyr Up.description:
Upsamples and blurs image (Gaussian pyramid up)

Params:
  dst_width (int): Output width (default: double) [optional]
  dst_height (int): Output height (default: double) [optional]

Example: pyr_up()

Rand Float.description:
Generates random float in range [min, max)

Params:
  min (float): Minimum value (inclusive)
  max (float): Maximum value (exclusive)
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: rand_float(0.0, 1.0, "random_val")

Rand Int.description:
Generates random integer in range [min, max]

Params:
  min (int): Minimum value (inclusive)
  max (int): Maximum value (inclusive)
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: rand_int(0, 100, "random_val")

Rand Shuffle.description:
Randomly shuffles matrix elements

Params:
  iter_factor (float): Iteration factor [optional]

Example: rand_shuffle(1.0)

Randn.description:
Fills matrix with normally distributed random values

Params:
  mean (float): Mean value [optional]
  stddev (float): Standard deviation [optional]

Example: randn(0, 50)

Randu.description:
Fills matrix with uniformly distributed random values

Params:
  low (float): Lower bound [optional]
  high (float): Upper bound [optional]

Example: randu(0, 255)

Read Control Panel.description:
Reads all control panel values into variables with prefix

Params:
  name (string): Control panel window name
  prefix (string): Variable prefix
  preset (string): Preset type used when creating

Example: read_control_panel("Controls", "ctrl", "threshold")

Recover Pose.description:
Recovers rotation and translation from essential matrix

Params:
  E_cache (string): Essential matrix cache
  points1_cache (string): First image points cache
  points2_cache (string): Second image points cache
  cam_matrix (string): Camera matrix cache
  cache_prefix (string): Output cache prefix [optional]

Example: recover_pose("E", "pts1", "pts2", "K", "pose")

Rectangle.description:
Draws a rectangle

Params:
  x (int): Top-left X
  y (int): Top-left Y
  width (int): Width
  height (int): Height
  color (string): Rectangle color [optional]
  thickness (int): Line thickness (-1 for filled) [optional]

Example: rectangle(10, 10, 100, 50, "red", 2)

Reduce.description:
Reduces matrix to vector by applying operation

Params:
  dim (int): Dimension to reduce: 0=rows, 1=cols
  rtype (string): Operation: sum, avg, max, min [optional]
  dtype (string): Output depth: -1, 32f, 64f [optional]

Example: reduce(0, "sum", "32f")

Remap.description:
Applies generic geometric transformation using lookup maps

Params:
  map1 (string): Cache ID of first map (x coordinates)
  map2 (string): Cache ID of second map (y coordinates)
  interpolation (string): Interpolation method [optional]
  border_mode (string): Border mode [optional]

Example: remap("map_x", "map_y")

Remove Small Objects.description:
Removes small connected components from binary image

Params:
  min_size (int): Minimum object area in pixels

Example: remove_small_objects(100)

Reproject Image To 3d.description:
Reprojects disparity image to 3D space

Params:
  q_cache (string): Q matrix cache ID
  handle_missing (bool): Handle missing values [optional]
  ddepth (string): Output depth: 32f, 16s [optional]

Example: reproject_image_to_3d("Q", true)

Reshape.description:
Changes mat shape without copying data

Params:
  channels (int): New number of channels
  rows (int): New number of rows (0 = auto) [optional]

Example: reshape(1, 0)

Resize.description:
Resizes the current frame

Params:
  width (int): Target width in pixels (0 = compute from scale)
  height (int): Target height in pixels (0 = compute from scale)
  interpolation (string): Interpolation: nearest, linear, cubic, area, lanczos, linear_exact [optional]
  fx (float): Scale factor along X (when width=0) [optional]
  fy (float): Scale factor along Y (when height=0) [optional]

Example: resize(640, 480) | resize(0, 0, "linear", 0.5, 0.5)

Resize Window.description:
Resizes window to specified size

Params:
  name (string): Window name
  width (int): Width
  height (int): Height

Example: resize_window("Output", 640, 480)

Roi.description:
Extracts region of interest from current mat

Params:
  x (int): X coordinate
  y (int): Y coordinate
  width (int): Width
  height (int): Height

Example: roi(100, 100, 200, 200)

Rotate.description:
Rotates image by an arbitrary angle

Params:
  angle (float): Rotation angle in degrees
  center_x (float): Center X (default: image center) [optional]
  center_y (float): Center Y (default: image center) [optional]
  scale (float): Scale factor [optional]
  border_mode (string): Border: constant, replicate, reflect, wrap [optional]
  border_value (int): Border color for constant mode [optional]

Example: rotate(45) | rotate(90, -1, -1, 1.0)

Rotate90.description:
Rotates image by 90, 180, or 270 degrees

Params:
  code (string): Rotation: 90, 180, 270, or cw, ccw, 180

Example: rotate90("90") | rotate90("cw")

Round Scalar.description:
Rounds to nearest integer

Params:
  value (float): Input value or variable name
  output_var (string): Output variable name

Example: round_scalar(3.7, "result")

Save Camera Profile.description:
Saves camera settings to a profile file

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  path (string): Profile file path (.xml or .json)

Example: save_camera_profile("0", "camera_profile.xml")

Save Image.description:
Saves the current frame to an image file

Params:
  filename (string): Output image file path
  quality (int): JPEG quality (0-100) or PNG compression (0-9) [optional]

Example: save_image("frame.png") | save_image("frame.jpg", 90)

Scalar Compare.description:
Compares two values and stores boolean result

Params:
  a (float): First value or variable name
  op (string): Operator: ==, !=, <, <=, >, >=
  b (float): Second value or variable name
  result_var (string): Variable name for result

Example: scalar_compare(threshold, ">", 100, "is_high")

Scalar In Range.description:
Checks if value is within range [min, max]

Params:
  value (float): Value to check or variable name
  min (float): Minimum value
  max (float): Maximum value
  result_var (string): Variable name for result

Example: scalar_in_range(threshold, 50, 200, "is_valid")

Scale Add.description:
Scaled addition: dst = scale*src1 + src2

Params:
  scale (float): Scale for current mat
  other_cache (string): Second image cache ID

Example: scale_add(0.5, "other")

Scharr.description:
Applies Scharr operator (more accurate than Sobel for 3x3)

Params:
  dx (int): Order of X derivative (0 or 1) [optional]
  dy (int): Order of Y derivative (0 or 1) [optional]
  scale (float): Scale factor [optional]
  delta (float): Value added to result [optional]

Example: scharr(1, 0) | scharr(0, 1)

Seamless Clone.description:
Seamlessly clones source into destination

Params:
  src_cache (string): Source image cache ID
  mask_cache (string): Mask cache ID
  center_x (int): Center X in destination
  center_y (int): Center Y in destination
  mode (string): Mode: normal, mixed, monochrome [optional]

Example: seamless_clone("src", "mask", 200, 150, "normal")

Select.description:
Sets variable to one of two values based on condition

Params:
  condition (bool): Boolean condition value or variable
  true_value (any): Value if condition is true
  false_value (any): Value if condition is false
  result_var (string): Variable name for result

Example: select(is_valid, 255, 0, "output_value")

Sep Filter2d.description:
Applies separable filter (faster for large kernels)

Params:
  kernel_x (array): Row kernel
  kernel_y (array): Column kernel
  ddepth (int): Output depth [optional]

Example: sep_filter2d([1,2,1], [1,2,1])

Set Camera Backend.description:
Sets camera backend API and buffer preferences

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  backend (string): Backend: dshow, msmf, v4l2, avfoundation, gstreamer, ffmpeg [optional]
  buffer_size (int): Buffer size (1-100 frames) [optional]

Example: set_camera_backend("0", "dshow", 1)

Set Camera Exposure.description:
Sets camera exposure mode and value

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  mode (string): Exposure mode: manual, auto, aperture_priority, shutter_priority [optional]
  value (float): Exposure value (-13 to 13 for manual, 0-1 for auto) [optional]

Example: set_camera_exposure("0", "manual", -5)

Set Camera Focus.description:
Sets camera focus mode and value

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  mode (string): Focus mode: auto, manual, continuous, infinity, macro [optional]
  value (int): Focus value (0-255, manual mode only) [optional]

Example: set_camera_focus("0", "manual", 200)

Set Camera Image Adjustment.description:
Sets camera image quality parameters

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  brightness (float): Brightness (-100 to 100) [optional]
  contrast (float): Contrast (-100 to 100) [optional]
  saturation (float): Saturation (-100 to 100) [optional]
  hue (float): Hue (-180 to 180) [optional]
  sharpness (float): Sharpness (0-100) [optional]
  gamma (float): Gamma (50-300) [optional]

Example: set_camera_image_adjustment("0", 10, 20, 0, 0, 60, 100)

Set Camera Iso.description:
Sets camera ISO/gain value

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  mode (string): ISO mode: auto, manual [optional]
  value (int): ISO value (100-6400) or gain (0-100) [optional]

Example: set_camera_iso("0", "manual", 800)

Set Camera Trigger.description:
Sets camera trigger and strobe controls

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  trigger_mode (string): Trigger mode: off, software, hardware [optional]
  trigger_delay (int): Delay in microseconds (0-1000000) [optional]
  strobe_enabled (bool): Enable strobe/flash [optional]

Example: set_camera_trigger("0", "software", 1000, false)

Set Camera White Balance.description:
Sets camera white balance mode and temperature

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  mode (string): WB mode: auto, manual, daylight, cloudy, tungsten, fluorescent [optional]
  temperature (int): Color temperature (2000-10000K) [optional]

Example: set_camera_white_balance("0", "manual", 6500)

Set Camera Zoom.description:
Sets camera zoom, pan, and tilt

Params:
  source (string): Camera source identifier
  zoom (float): Zoom level (1.0-10.0) [optional]
  pan (float): Pan angle (-180 to 180) [optional]
  tilt (float): Tilt angle (-90 to 90) [optional]

Example: set_camera_zoom("0", 2.5, 30, 0)

Set If.description:
Sets variable only if condition is true

Params:
  condition (bool): Boolean condition value or variable
  var_name (string): Variable name to set
  value (any): Value to set

Example: set_if(is_valid, "threshold", 128)

Set To.description:
Sets mat pixels to specified value

Params:
  value (float): Scalar value to set
  mask_cache (string): Optional mask cache ID [optional]

Example: set_to(0, "mask")

Set Trackbar From Var.description:
Sets trackbar position from a variable value

Params:
  name (string): Trackbar name
  window (string): Window name
  var_name (string): Variable name containing value

Example: set_trackbar_from_var("Threshold", "Control", "thresh")

Set Trackbar Pos.description:
Sets trackbar position

Params:
  name (string): Trackbar name
  window (string): Window name
  pos (int): Position value

Example: set_trackbar_pos("Threshold", "Output", 128)

Set Var.description:
Sets a variable in the execution context

Params:
  name (string): Variable name
  value (float): Variable value

Example: set_var("threshold", 127)

Set Video Prop.description:
Sets a video capture property

Params:
  source (string): Video source identifier
  prop (string): Property name
  value (float): Property value

Example: set_video_prop("0", "exposure", -5)

Set Window Title.description:
Sets window title

Params:
  name (string): Window name
  title (string): New title

Example: set_window_title("Output", "Processed Image")

Sharpen.description:
Applies unsharp masking to sharpen the image

Params:
  amount (float): Sharpening strength (0-3) [optional]
  radius (int): Blur radius for mask [optional]
  threshold (int): Threshold for sharpening (0-255) [optional]

Example: sharpen(1.5) | sharpen(1.0, 3)

Shm Create.description:
Create a named POSIX shared-memory region for inter-process frame exchange (double-buffered).  Call once during setup.

Params:
  name (string): Shared-memory region name
  width (int): Frame width in pixels
  height (int): Frame height in pixels
  channels (int): Number of channels (1=gray, 3=BGR) [optional]

Example: shm_create("left_cam", 1640, 1232, 3)

Shm Read.description:
Read the latest frame from a named shared-memory region.  Returns the most recent frame published by a shm_write in another process (e.g. an exec_fork capture pipeline).

Params:
  name (string): Shared-memory region name

Stabilization Apply.description:
Apply a pre-computed stabilization correction matrix to the current frame. Accepts the 2×3 affine matrix returned by stabilization_calculate() and warps the current frame with it. Optionally scales the result to hide the black borders introduced by the warp.

Params:
  movement_matrix (mat): 2×3 CV_64F correction matrix from stabilization_calculate(). Omit or pass an unset variable on the first frame for a no-op pass-through. [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop to hide warp borders (default true) [optional]

Example: movement_matrix = stabilization_calculate()
stabilization_apply(movement_matrix)
// apply the same transform to a different frame:
use("other_cam") | stabilization_apply(movement_matrix)

body:
// single camera:
movement_matrix = stabilization_calculate(${1:)
stabilization_apply(movement_matrix)
// multi-camera with independent state:
matrix_l = stabilization_calculate("cam_l"}, ${2:0.8}, ${3:0.6)
matrix_r = stabilization_calculate("cam_r"}, ${4:0.8}, ${5:0.6)
use("other_cam"})
Stabilization Calculate.description:
Compute the 2-D stabilization correction transform without applying it. Tracks feature points and accumulates the camera-motion trajectory (Δx, Δy, Δθ) via Lucas-Kanade / RANSAC, then returns the 2×3 affine counter-transform matrix as a MAT value. The current frame passes through unchanged. Pass the returned matrix to stabilization_apply() to warp any frame with it.

Params:
  id (string): Stream identifier — unique string per camera so each stream keeps independent state (default "default") [optional]
  delta_smooth (float): EMA coefficient for per-frame delta noise [0,1) (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame decay in (0,1]. 1.0 = hard lock (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality level (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min distance between corners in px (default 10) [optional]
  reset (bool): Reset accumulated state and restart (default false) [optional]

Example: // single camera:
movement_matrix = stabilization_calculate()
stabilization_apply(movement_matrix)
// multi-camera with independent state:
matrix_l = stabilization_calculate("cam_l", 0.8, 0.6)
matrix_r = stabilization_calculate("cam_r", 0.8, 0.6)
use("other_cam") | stabilization_apply(matrix_r)

Stereo Stabilize.description:
Stereo-synchronised 2-D video stabilizer: reads left frame from the pipeline (currentMat) and right frame from cache, tracks feature points in both views, estimates a single shared correction transform (Δx, Δy, Δθ) from the pooled matches, and applies the identical counter-warp to both frames. This preserves epipolar geometry for downstream stereo matching.

Params:
  right_cache (string): Cache key of the right frame
  output_cache (string): Cache key for the stabilised right frame (default "stabilized_right") [optional]
  delta_smooth (float): EMA coefficient for per-frame delta noise [0,1) (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame decay ∈ (0,1]. 1.0 = hard lock (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners per view (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality level (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min corner distance in px (default 10) [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop to hide borders (default true) [optional]
  reset (bool): Clear state and restart (default false) [optional]

Example: use("rect_l")
stereo_stabilize("rect_r", "stab_r")
// currentMat = stabilised left, cache "stab_r" = stabilised right

V4l2 Prop.description:

When the control is not found on the video device, the manager automatically discovers and tries linked sub-devices via the media controller (BFS upstream traversal, sensor first).
Pass an explicit subdev path as the 4th argument to bypass auto-discovery.

Params:
  source (any): Device path ("/dev/video0", "/dev/v4l-subdev28") or index
  control_name (string): Control name, case-insensitive with space/underscore equivalence (e.g. "Exposure", "vertical_flip", "analogue_gain")
  value (float): Control value
  subdev (string): Explicit /dev/v4l-subdev* path to target directly, bypassing auto-discovery. Useful on ISP pipelines where the video node is an intermediate VFE. [optional]

Example: v4l2_prop("/dev/video0", "vertical_flip", 1)
v4l2_prop("/dev/video3", "vertical_flip", 1, "/dev/v4l-subdev28")
v4l2_prop("/dev/v4l-subdev28", "exposure", 800)

  subdev (string): Pin a specific /dev/v4l-subdev* path for control lookup on this device. Skips media-controller BFS on complex ISP pipelines (e.g. Qualcomm MSM). Leave empty for auto-discovery. [optional]
  camera_device_manager_id (string): Bind this camera to a named device manager instance. Cameras on different managers have independent mutexes, eliminating cross-device lock contention and enabling true parallel capture throughput. [optional]

Example: v4l2_setup("/dev/video0", 1920, 1080, "SRGGB10", 30)

Create a vector of ones

Example: vec_ones(10)

Vec Range.description:
Create a vector with range of values

Params:

  end (float): End value (exclusive)
  step (float): Step size [optional]

Example: vec_range(0, 10) | vec_range(0, 10, 2)

Vec Set.description:
Set vector element at index

Params:

Example: vec_set(v, 0, 5.0)

Vec Slice.description:
Get vector slice

Params:

  start (int): Start index

Example: vec_slice(v, 0, 5)

Vec Zeros.description:
Create a zero vector

Params:

Video Cap.description:
Captures video frames from a source (camera, file, or URL)

Params:
  source (any): Source: device index, file path, or URL
  api (string): API backend: auto, dshow, v4l2, ffmpeg, libcamera, v4l2_native, gstreamer [optional]
  format (string): Capture format: MJPG, UYVY, NV12, etc. [optional]

Example: video_cap(0) | video_cap("video.mp4")

Video Close.description:
Closes a video capture or writer

Params:
  source (string): Source identifier to close (empty = all) [optional]

Example: video_close("0") | video_close()

Video Seek.description:
Seeks to a specific position in a video file

Params:
  source (string): Video source identifier
  position (float): Position (frame number or msec)
  unit (string): Position unit: frames, msec, ratio [optional]

Example: video_seek("video.mp4", 100, "frames")

Video Stabilize.description:
Full 2-D video stabilizer: cancels translational shake AND rotational jitter in a single warp. Tracks feature points, estimates Δx/Δy/Δθ each frame via RANSAC partial-affine, noise-filters them with an EMA, integrates into an accumulated trajectory, and counter-transforms the frame with no lag and no overshoot.

Params:
  delta_smooth (float): EMA coefficient for per-frame delta noise filter [0,1). Higher = smoother (default 0.5) [optional]
  drift_decay (float): Per-frame decay of accumulated motion (0,1]. 1.0 = hard lock; ~0.997 = soft lock ~11s (default 0.997) [optional]
  max_points (int): Max Shi-Tomasi corners to track (default 200) [optional]
  quality (float): Shi-Tomasi quality level (default 0.01) [optional]
  min_distance (float): Min distance between corners in px (default 10) [optional]
  crop (bool): Scale-crop to hide warp borders (default true) [optional]
  reset (bool): Reset accumulated state and restart (default false) [optional]

Example: video_cap("/dev/video0") | video_stabilize()
video_cap("/dev/video0") | video_stabilize(0.5, 0.997)
// hard lock — no drift allowed:
video_cap("/dev/video0") | video_stabilize(0.5, 1.0)

body:
# Sync /dev/video9 to match /dev/video3(${1:works with MIPI sub-device controls):
video_sync("/dev/video3"}, ${2:/dev/video9")

# With verbose logging:
video_sync("/dev/video3}, ${3:/dev/video9}, ${4:verbose=true})
Video Sync.description:
Copies V4L2 controls from a master camera device to a target device so
both cameras run with identical hardware settings.

Sub-device aware: enumerates controls from the video node AND every linked
sub-device (sensor, ISP, etc.) discovered via the media controller, so
sensor-level controls such as 'exposure' and 'analogue_gain' that live on
/dev/v4l-subdev* are included even when the device path is /dev/video*.

Cached two-phase operation:
  First call  — discovery: enumerate all readable controls on master,
                test-write each to target (marks writable/not-writable),
                perform initial full sync.
  Later calls — delta: read each tracked control from master; write to
                target only when the value has changed since the last sync.
                Zero ioctls on a stable scene.

Returns the number of controls written to target on this call.

Params:
  master (any): Master device: path ("/dev/video3") or index. Controls are READ from here.
  target (any): Target device: path ("/dev/video9") or index. Controls are WRITTEN here.
  verbose (bool): Log each control name and value as it is copied (default: false) [optional]

Example: # Sync /dev/video9 to match /dev/video3 (works with MIPI sub-device controls):
video_sync("/dev/video3", "/dev/video9")

# With verbose logging:
video_sync("/dev/video3", "/dev/video9", verbose=true)

Video Write.description:
Writes frames to a video file

Params:
  filename (string): Output video file path
  fps (float): Frames per second [optional]
  fourcc (string): FourCC codec code [optional]
  is_color (bool): Write as color video [optional]

Example: video_write("output.avi", 30, "MJPG")

Vstack.description:
Stacks images vertically

Params:

Example: vstack("bottom_image")

Wait Key.description:
Waits for a key press and returns the key code

Params:

Example: key = wait_key(30)

Warp Affine.description:
Applies affine transformation

Params:

  height (int): Output height (default: same as input) [optional]
  interpolation (string): Interpolation method [optional]
  border_mode (string): Border mode [optional]

Example: warp_affine("transform_matrix")

Warp Perspective.description:

Weighted Gray.description:

Zeros.description:
Creates a zero-filled mat

Params:
